skill.name: surrealdb-python
skill.description: Guidance for using SurrealDB 2.3.x with Python across CRUD, graph relationships, vector search, live queries, and RAG workflows.
skill.summary: Build SurrealDB Python applications with graph, vector, real-time, and RAG patterns.
skill.author: ActiveInferenceInstitute

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-cryptography detections appear to be false positives caused by markdown code fences, SurrealQL arrow syntax, KNN notation, and similarity terminology. The skill is documentation-only and shows no prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent, but several examples use plaintext localhost connections and default root credentials that could be copied into unsafe deployments.

medium_findings.title: Insecure Example Database Credentials
medium_findings.description: The documentation examples connect to ws://localhost:8000/rpc and authenticate with root/root credentials. This is acceptable for local demonstrations, but it creates a production copy-paste risk if users do not replace credentials and transport settings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credentials and plaintext local WebSocket URL are directly visible in examples. The risk is moderate because the surrounding context is instructional and localhost-focused, not malicious.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick and arrow patterns are markdown code fences, inline code, Python examples, and SurrealQL graph syntax. No shell command execution API or user-controlled command construction was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced Python or SQL documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution. A targeted search found no subprocess, os.system, eval, or exec pattern in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts align with database and vector-search terminology such as KNN, similarity, model names, and markdown metadata. No cryptographic algorithm implementation or security-sensitive hashing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe SurrealDB capabilities and vector search concepts. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic primitive being used.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the SurrealDB Python repository, PyPI package, official documentation, and SurrealQL reference. These are passive documentation links, not runtime callbacks or data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are listed under Additional Resources and are not used by executable code. They reference expected official project and package resources.

dangerous_patterns.title: Default Credentials in Copyable Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Copyable examples use root/root database credentials. Users should replace these with least-privilege accounts and secure transport before using the patterns outside local development.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples plainly include default credentials. The context limits confidence in severe impact because they target localhost demonstrations.

content.value_statement: SurrealDB projects need correct patterns for graph, vector, and live queries. This skill gives Python-focused examples for schemas, queries, embeddings, and RAG workflows.
actual_capabilities: Shows Python SDK connection, authentication, namespace, and database setup patterns.
Explains CRUD operations using create, select, update, merge, patch, upsert, delete, and query methods.
Provides SurrealQL graph relationship examples with RELATE, traversal syntax, recursive queries, and edge metadata.
Demonstrates vector storage, KNN search, similarity scoring, hybrid search, and embedding generation patterns.
Covers live query subscriptions for real-time create, update, and delete notifications.
Includes reference material for RAG workflows and LangChain-style vector store usage.
limitations: Examples are documentation patterns and are not a packaged application or tested project template.
Users must provide their own SurrealDB instance, credentials, schema choices, and deployment configuration.
Embedding examples depend on external libraries or services that may require separate installation and API access.
Some examples use local root credentials and should be changed before production use.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Multi-Model Data Layer
use_cases.description: Use the examples to design document tables, graph relationships, and vector fields in one SurrealDB database.

use_cases.title: Build a Semantic Search Workflow
use_cases.description: Follow the embedding and KNN examples to store document vectors and retrieve relevant content for search or RAG.

use_cases.title: Model Connected Research Data
use_cases.description: Apply RELATE and traversal patterns to represent authors, articles, concepts, citations, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up a Connection
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a local SurrealDB Python project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me connect a Python application to SurrealDB, select a namespace and database, and run a simple create and select workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Design Graph Relationships
prompt_templates.scenario: Create graph tables and traversal queries.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a SurrealDB graph schema for my entities and relationships. Include RELATE examples, edge metadata, and traversal queries.

prompt_templates.title: Add Vector Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to store embeddings in SurrealDB and query the nearest documents with metadata filtering and similarity scores.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a RAG Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow combining chunking, embeddings, graph context, and retrieval.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SurrealDB Python RAG design that combines document chunks, vector search, graph relationships, and real-time updates.

output_examples.input: Ask for a SurrealDB Python setup plan.
output_examples.output: A concise setup sequence covering connection handling, authentication, namespace selection, table creation, and a first CRUD workflow.

output_examples.input: Ask how to model article citations and authors.
output_examples.output: A graph design with entity tables, relationship tables, edge metadata, traversal ideas, and integrity recommendations.

output_examples.input: Ask for a semantic search approach.
output_examples.output: A retrieval plan covering embeddings, vector fields, KNN search, scoring, metadata filters, chunking, and production cautions.

best_practices: Use parameterized queries and avoid building SurrealQL from untrusted strings.
Replace local root credentials with least-privilege accounts before shared or production use.
Define schemas, indexes, assertions, and query timeouts for predictable production behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not copy root/root credentials into deployed applications.
Do not run unbounded recursive graph traversals without depth limits or timeouts.
Do not store embeddings without consistent dimensions, source metadata, and a retrieval strategy.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps build Python applications that use SurrealDB for documents, graph relationships, vector search, live queries, and RAG patterns.

faq.question: Does it include runnable project files?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation examples and reference patterns that users adapt to their own project structure.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require a SurrealDB server?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples assume access to a SurrealDB instance and valid authentication details.

faq.question: Does it cover vector search?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers embedding generation, vector storage, KNN queries, similarity scoring, hybrid search, and RAG retrieval patterns.

faq.question: What security caution matters most?
faq.answer: Replace local root credentials and plaintext local connection examples before using the patterns in shared or production environments.
